summary: Iteration 42: Full E2E regression on all 10 auth journeys for FREE11 IPL 2026 app. All 10 tests PASSED. Covers: RootRoute redirect (auth/unauth), Registration via OTP, Login via Password, Login via EMAIL OTP, Google button existence, Auth persistence on refresh, and redirect behaviors.

ui_bugs.issue: React warning: 'Each child in a list should have a unique key prop' in render method. Non-blocking but causes console noise.

issues: dev-otp-display only appears when dev_otp is in the send-otp API response (i.e., when Resend email is NOT configured). In production (email_delivered:true), no dev_otp is returned and the gold box is not shown - this is correct behavior by design.

Login.js sendOtp() does NOT read the response body (no res.json() call), so even if dev_otp were in the response, the UI would NOT show it. Unlike Register.js which has the dev-otp-display, Login.js has no dev_otp fallback display. This is a design gap - testers in dev environments will need to read OTP from MongoDB logs when testing Login OTP mode.

passed_tests.details: Visiting '/' while not logged in shows Landing page (stays at '/'), does NOT redirect to /dashboard
Visiting '/' after login correctly redirects to /dashboard (RootRoute component works)

passed_tests.test: 3. REGISTRATION FLOW - New user via OTP
passed_tests.details: Navigate /register → enter unique email → Send OTP → dev-otp-display visible and clickable → auto-fills 6 boxes → Verify & Join → /dashboard. Token stored in localStorage.
PASSWORD tab is gold/selected by default → fill credentials → Sign In → /match-centre
Click EMAIL OTP tab → otp-login-section shown → fill email → Send OTP → otp-boxes + resend timer appear → fill OTP → Verify & Sign In → /match-centre
google-login-btn present with 'Continue with Google' text. auth.emergentagent.com referenced in page source for OAuth redirect.
After login, visiting '/' → redirected to /dashboard (RootRoute with auth user)
Navigating to /login while authenticated triggers useEffect → navigate('/match-centre'). URL changes to /match-centre.
google-login-btn present on /register with 'Continue with Google' text
Login → token in localStorage → page.reload() → token still present → URL remains /match-centre (not redirected to /login)

action_items: LOW: Fix React 'key' prop warning in Dashboard.js list renders

INFO: Login.js sendOtp() does not parse API response body - cannot show dev_otp fallback like Register.js. Consider adding dev_otp display to Login.js for improved developer experience in environments where Resend is not configured.
critical_code_review_comments: Dashboard.js has missing React 'key' props in list renders - minor code quality issue

Login.js sendOtp() does not call res.json() - means dev_otp from backend is silently discarded. This is fine for production (email delivered) but for dev environment testing you need to read OTP from MongoDB. Consider adding the same dev_otp display pattern from Register.js for parity.
Note: The verify-otp-register endpoint (used by BOTH Register.js and Login.js OTP mode) correctly handles existing users (login) vs new users (register) in the backend.
seed_data_creation: Created test user testuser_e2e_clean_1773140477@test.com via registration OTP flow during Test 3.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 10 auth E2E tests pass. Key notes: (1) Production has Resend email configured (email_delivered:true), so dev_otp is NOT shown in Register.js - need MongoDB access to get OTP for fresh user tests. (2) Login.js EMAIL OTP mode works but does NOT show dev_otp display (unlike Register.js) - Login.js sendOtp() doesn't parse the response body. (3) RootRoute in App.js correctly waits for AuthContext loading before deciding redirect. (4) All data-testid attributes are present and working. (5) Tested with password login, OTP register, OTP login, root redirect (auth/unauth), google button, refresh persistence, already-logged-in redirect.
